g.text: Context peaks at end of list (recency). Strong early bindings (φᵢ)

text.font-family: Inter, Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

g.text: lift "king" above middle items → U-shaped curve. βstart reinstates c₀.

text.font-family: Inter, Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
Inter, Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

g.text: βrec reinstates context via MFC → neighbors win.

text.font-family: Inter, Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

g.text: Semantic similarity s·S adds activation (purple), not context.

text.font-family: Inter, Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

g.text: this cycle repeats: each recall shifts context and cues the next item

text.font-family: Inter, Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

g.text: TERMINATION Successive recalls become less productive and search eventually stops.

text.font-family: Inter, Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif

g.text: P(recall i) = (1 − Pstop) · Aiτ / Σk Akτ τ : choice sensitivity

text.font-family: Inter, Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif

g.text: P(stop, j) = θs · ej · θr θₛ : stop probability scale, θᵣ : growth rate

text.font-family: Inter, Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

g.text: Core insight: recalling an item brings back its encoding context, and that reinstated context guides what you remember next.

text.font-family: Inter, Helvetica Neue, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

svg.text: Morton & Polyn (2016). A predictive framework for evaluating models of semantic organization in free recall. J. Memory & Language.
